title: Create and Maintain Glyphs
text: Create and Maintain Glyphs Glyphs are maintained and deployed in a runtime library. Using your preferred graphical tools, create one or more images to use as sources for the glyph items. For more information, see Glyphs . Open the glyph editor: Choose More Editors > Glyphs . Select or create the library to hold the glyph. If the library does not yet contain glyphs, the Insert Glyph dialog is displayed. You can choose to immediately define a new glyph or click Cancel to go to the glyph editor. Figure 1. Glyph Editor To create a glyph: Click New in the glyph editor (Define Glyphs). The Insert Glyph dialog is displayed. Click the >> button beside the Primary File , and select the image created as the primary source. Uniface automatically detects the size and colors of the image. Figure 2. Insert Glyph Optionally, do the same for the Secondary File to load a secondary version of the image. You can create multiple items for each glyph. At run time Uniface displays the variant that is appropriate to the environment, for example, a 16-bit color glyph instead of a 32-bit color glyph. Ensure that the Display check box is selected for each source file specified, otherwise the glyph will not be displayed at runtime. Click OK to load the images and return to the glyph editor. Uniface generates a glyph name by prefixing the primary image name with U_ , and puts the original file name in the Description . Edit the Glyph Name and Language . To compile and save the glyph, choose File > Save . Alternatively, click OK to compile and store the glyph, and close the editor. Note: After the glyph has been defined and stored, it is not possible to change its properties. If you want to do so, you must delete it and redefine it. For more information, see Modifying Glyphs . To delete a glyph, select it and choose Edit > Delete Glyph . Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
